Aggregated bias · 1-glance synthesis
Today's verdict NEUTRAL-CAUTIOUS
When the signals disagree · and which one to trust
Different gauges sometimes point opposite ways. That is normal. Here is each clash in plain words, with the side to lean on for now.
⚠ These two disagree: Crash Index **3/10** WARNING but still low on price stress vs VIX Spike tier **2/4** MEDIUM with **60%** probability, skew active, backwardation active
Price action is calm while the options market is paying up for downside and an inverted VIX curve; trust the VIX complex here because skew plus backwardation lead realized stress, the Crash Index follows.
⚠ These two disagree: Regime State **36** STABLE with MIXED bias vs Divergence composite ELEVATED, **48.8%** of pairs diverging, net bias **+13** bearish
Headline regime score reads quiet while internal cross-asset correlations are breaking; trust the divergence panel because correlation breaks lead regime-score moves by weeks.
⚠ These two disagree: Contrarian score **43** ACCUMULATE partial signal vs Divergence ELEVATED with **17** bearish pairs vs **4** bullish out of **43**
The contrarian model is leaning into weakness while internals are still deteriorating; trust the divergence read for timing, the contrarian score for sizing into eventual washouts.
Today's tension
Options markets are pricing fear while price action stays calm and breadth quietly broadens.
Bull
The Crash Index sits at **3/10** with the regime state at **36** and labeled STABLE, and the macro regime read is BROADENING, which is what you want to see if leadership is rotating out of mega-cap tech into the rest of the book.
Bear
VIX Spike sits at tier **2/4** MEDIUM with a **60%** probability, skew is active and the curve is in backwardation, and the divergence composite is ELEVATED with **48.8%** of pairs diverging and a **+13** net bearish bias.
The tell: Watch whether VIX backwardation clears or pairs with HYG widening; the T1 x HYG confluence flipping active would resolve the standoff toward the bear.
AI read · Citrini · today's macro tape

You have a tape that looks calm on the surface and stressed underneath, and that is the whole trade today. Crash Index is 3/10 and regime state is 36 STABLE, yet VIX Spike sits at tier 2/4 MEDIUM with 60% probability, skew active, and the VIX curve in backwardation. The macro regime is BROADENING at 30% confidence, the contrarian model reads 43 ACCUMULATE partial, and the divergence composite is ELEVATED with 48.8% of 43 pairs diverging and a +13 net bearish bias. Price says nothing is wrong. The options market and the internals say something is shifting.

Position for broadening, not for chasing. NDX leads the US at +3.2% over five days while the Dow lags at +1.3%, so the rotation story is alive but mega-cap tech is still doing the heavy lifting. With contrarian at 43 and macro regime BROADENING, you accumulate into equal-weight and laggards on weakness rather than pressing the cap-weighted index here. Keep gross modest because VIX backwardation and active skew tell you the hedging bid is real, and protection is cheap to carry when the curve is already inverted.

The risk that flips you defensive is a credit crack. None of the three flagship confluences (T1 x HYG, T1 x B2, T1 x JPY) are active, so the vol stress has not married to credit or small-caps or yen safe-haven flow yet. The single reading that flips the stance is T1 x HYG going active: VIX backwardation paired with HYG widening would turn this from a rotation tape into a risk-off tape, and you would cut equity gross and add duration immediately.

5-regime heuristic classifier · cross-asset
Macro Regime — where are we structurally
AI read · macro regime

Macro regime reads BROADENING at 30% confidence, which is the right backdrop for rotation but not for blind beta. Broadening means leadership is expanding beyond a narrow group of winners into the rest of the market, and the SPY vs RSP correlation drift from -0.18 to +0.86 confirms equal-weight is finally catching the cap-weight tape.

Position the book with overweight equal-weight and cyclicals against an underweight in the crowded mega-cap longs, and keep beta moderate because confidence is only 30%. Use the contrarian 43 ACCUMULATE read as your add-on-weakness trigger for laggards, not as a green light to press gross.

SPY above its 200-DMA with contained vol. This is the zone where long convexity compounds: leverage and call buying carry the lowest variance drag. Size up your highest-conviction directional calls here.
Gate from Leverage for the Long Run (Gayed/Bilello 2016): the durable edge is the 200-DMA × vol regime, the 3× compounding is marketing. Attention gauge for call sizing, not a trade trigger.

Dow Theory generalized · cross-asset divergences
Cross-asset divergence radar — Cross-asset divergence watch
AI read · divergences in plain English

Cross-asset correlations are breaking in ways that matter. The divergence composite sits in the ELEVATED zone with 48.8% of 43 pairs diverging and a net bias of +13 toward bearish (17 bearish pairs vs 4 bullish).

Mother Jaws composite reads 100. In plain English, a divergence happens when two assets that normally move together stop doing so, and correlation drift is the change in that relationship over a defined window.

The top jaws today: SPY vs RSP drifts from -0.18 to +0.86 (cap-weight and equal-weight have re-synced, which fits the broadening regime), LQD vs IEF drifts from -0.09 to +0.88 (investment-grade credit is now tracking duration tightly, so any back-up in yields hits credit directly), and HG vs GC drifts from +0.04 to +0.84 (copper and gold both rallying, which is unusual because copper is cyclical and gold is defensive). The 2s10s pair has flipped from +0.67 to -0.57, a meaningful curve signal.

Read this as the market repricing the rate path and the cycle at the same time, with internals warning that the next regime is being built underneath a quiet index.

Cross-asset confluence · live status
Confluences — most proven alpha of the framework 0/3 ACTIVE
AI read · what the confluences mean now

Zero of three flagship confluences are active today (T1 x HYG, T1 x B2, T1 x JPY all dark). VIX backwardation is on, but credit (HYG) is not stressed, small-caps (B2) are not in drawdown, and yen safe-haven flow (JPY) is absent.

You lean cautiously constructive because the vol-stress signal has not married to a second risk channel; isolated VIX backwardation without credit or small-cap or yen confirmation reads as positioning churn, not a regime change. Keep your eye on HYG specifically.

If that pair lights up you have the cleanest possible setup to flip defensive, and you should act on the first one that activates rather than waiting for all three.

✅
T1 × HYG (VIX backwardation + Credit stress)
Volatility backwardation meeting credit stress — the highest-conviction pre-correction setup in the framework. Fires only when options fear and the bond market confirm each other.

🌍
Asia · Europe · US · Crypto 24/7 — lead-lag detection
Cross-Region Surveillance
Major indices from the 4 regions/asset classes. Asia trades ahead time-wise vs US — a Tokyo overnight crash often leaks into the SPX open. Crypto = 24h leading indicator of global risk appetite.
AI read · the cross-region tape

Risk is on outside the US and the US tape is leading from tech, which sets up a continuation bid into the cash open. Asia is split with ASX +0.1% holding while KOSPI bleeds -7.9% over five days, a Korea-specific air pocket worth isolating before extrapolating to regional risk.

Europe is firm with SMI +2.7% leading and DAX -2.0% lagging, a defensive-over-cyclical lead that does not match the US broadening narrative and is the cleanest tell that European internals are weaker than the US ones. The US itself shows NDX +3.2% ahead of DJI +1.3%, so tech is still pulling the index.

Crypto has SOL +8.8% leading while BTC is -2.0%, a risk-seeking rotation within the 24/7 tape that argues for an early-session bid in high-beta US growth. The KOSPI drawdown and the DAX lag are the two warnings you carry into the US open.

Forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
Crash Index — 10 lead-signal radar 3/10 · WARNING — 3+ signals, active surveillance
AI read · volatility & crash, reconciled

Calm price, paid-up options: this is a coherent state, not a contradiction. Crash Index at 3/10 measures realized stress across price-based signals and it is low because nothing has actually broken yet.

VIX Spike at tier 2/4 MEDIUM with 60% probability, active skew, and active backwardation measures what the options market is paying to hedge forward, and that complex is pricing a vol expansion. Skew active means out-of-the-money puts are bid relative to calls (dealers and funds are paying up for downside).

Backwardation active means near-term VIX futures trade above longer-dated ones, which only happens when the market expects stress now, not later. The adaptive trigger is off so this is not yet a full spike setup, but you trust the options market here over the calm tape because skew and curve shape lead realized vol by days to weeks.

Practical read: own some convexity, do not chase index upside with naked length.

Plain-English read · the Crash Index
Think of this as a smoke alarm, not a market forecast. Each of the 10 rows is a signal that led real crashes, checked over 15 years of S&P 500 history (Bonferroni-validated, so it survived a strict false-positive test). In a calm market it sits near zero by design — a low number is the alarm working, not a broken gauge. It only earns your attention when 3 or more rows light up together. Today it reads 3/10 — a few early signals are stirring; watch whether more line up.

3/10

Score 0-2 CALM · 3-4 WATCH · 5-6 WARNING · 7-8 ALARM · 9-10 EXTREME (rare crash imminent). Lead times: structural 30-90d · stress 5-30d · triggers 1-10d. Four Telegram alerts available depending on escalation.
How to read
Each row = an independent signal Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY. The structural (DXY, SKEW, MOVE) signal a regime change over 30-90 days. The stress (HYG/IEF, VIX3M/VIX, STLFSI4) ring at 5-30 days. The triggers (SPY -2% in 5d, VIX +30% in 3d) are short-term breakouts. For a tactical decision: 3+ signals simultaneously aligned = real probability of stress, never on isolated signal (framework rule).

Action — sizing tied to tier
Buy hedges B-tier (1-2% book) — VIX calls 30-60 DTE strike 25-28 OR SPY puts 60-90 DTE Δ0.20-0.30
How to read
4 tiers (BROAD / MEDIUM / HIGH / EXTREME) based on the confluence of 3 Validated components: SKEW > 140 (tail-hedge demand), VIX3M/VIX > 1.05 (vol curve inverted), VIX < 16 + ratio > 1.15 (adaptive proxy). Each tier indicates a historical probability of VIX +50% at 60d (cycle backtest 2023-2026, vs baseline 37%). Strict sizing: tier 2 = 1-2% book hedges, tier 3 = 3-5% long-vol, tier 4 = 5-10% MAX (rare confluence).

Aggregated bias · 53-ratio composite
Regime synthesis — RISK-ON / OFF / STRESS / TOPPING
MIXED — competing signals No clean composite per Kerberos. Wait for alignment.
RISK-ON aligned (3)
• Small caps leading
• Copper > Gold
• Industrials > Utilities
RISK-OFF aligned (1)
• ETH dumping vs BTC
STRESS aligned (2)
• Tail-risk premium spiking
• Dollar wrecking ball
TOPPING aligned (0)
none
How to read
Aggregation of all panel signals across the 4 cross-asset buckets. RISK-ON = risk appetite (cyclical, equity, EM). RISK-OFF = flight to defensives (TLT, gold, JPY). STRESS = liquidity tension (HYG, MOVE, STLFSI). TOPPING = end-of-cycle signals (concentration, breadth narrowing, defensives leading). Doctrine: a single signal = noise. 3+ aligned in one bucket = real confirmed regime.

EV% = win_rate × (target−entry) − (1−win_rate) × (entry−stop). Tier = T1 highest conviction. 52w% = % of 52-week high. Entry/stop/target levels live on the Trade Desk →
How to read
Heat ranking: composite of RS (relative strength vs SPY), Momentum (price action 20-60d), Breadth (% stocks above SMA), Options flow, and Attention. Use top-5 as rotation focus.
Emerging industries: high velocity = screener scores accelerating this week vs prior baseline — potential early rotation signal.
Competitor themes: token frequency × recency across top finance Substacks — useful narrative context, not a trading signal.
Best R/R: only entries with an active screener leg (PULLBACK / BOTTOM / SMART_MONEY_SURGE etc.). EV% = probabilistic edge per trade. Cross-check with weekly chart before sizing.

Reading the table — Z-Score: number of standard deviations vs rolling 100d mean. |Z| > 2 = Kerberos entry threshold, |Z| > 2.5 = extreme (best R/R on mean-reversion). %B: position within Bollinger Bands (1.0 = upper band, 0 = lower band).

Aggregate reading — the mean of Z-scores gives a global regime indicator. Positive → cross-asset overbought market (Risk-On, watch reversal). Negative → oversold (Risk-Off, possible bottoming).
How to read
Persist = number of consecutive days above |Z| > 2.0 (Kerberos threshold). Persist ≥ 3d = validated signal (the divergence has become a regime, not a spike). Persist 1-2d = to watch. Persist 0d = dead signal. Sort on this column to find established regimes worth trading.
Glossary — 18 terms to understand the dashboard
Z-Score
Number of standard deviations vs the historical mean (rolling 100 days). Z=0 = normal level · |Z|>2 = stress · |Z|>2.5 = extreme.
Bollinger Bands
Bands drawn at ±2.2σ around a 100-day moving average. When the price exits the bands = statistically extreme signal.
Backwardation
Inverted curve: immediate fear exceeds far fear (e.g. VIX > VIX3M). Short-term panic signal.
Contango
Normal situation where far fear exceeds immediate fear. Extreme contango = complacency.
Half-life
Number of days required for a gap to the mean to halve. Measures the speed of mean-reversion.
Cointegration
Two assets that move together in the long term despite short-term fluctuations. Statistical basis of pair-trading.
Mean-reversion
Strategy betting that a gap to the mean will close. Inverse of momentum (which bets on continuation).
Breadth
Width of participation in the rally. Good breadth = many stocks rising. Bad breadth = only a few mega-caps pull the index.
Hedge ratio
Quantity of asset B to short to neutralize 1 long unit of asset A. Calculated via OLS regression on historical returns.
Lead-time
Delay between a signal's appearance and the event it predicts. Structural = 30-90d · stress = 5-30d · triggers = 1-10d.
Bonferroni
Statistical correction for multiple testing. When testing 100 signals, some seem valid by chance. Bonferroni divides the significance threshold by the number of tests to eliminate these false positives.
MCPT (Monte Carlo Permutation Test)
Robustness test: data is randomly shuffled 5000-10000 times to see if the signal appears by chance. If p < 0.05, the signal is probably real.
Walk-forward
Test where the model is trained on the first years (e.g. 2010-2020) and tested on the following years (2020-2025). Avoids in-sample overfitting.
Tail-hedge
Hedge against an extreme crash (-20% or more). Bought via deeply out-of-the-money puts. SKEW measures institutional demand for this type of insurance.
Mega-caps
The 7-10 largest market caps in the SPX (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA). Weigh ~35% of the index.
Cap-weighted vs Equal-weighted
SPY = weighted by capitalization (mega-caps dominate). RSP = each stock weighs 0.2%. Comparing both reveals concentration.
Risk parity
Portfolio strategy that balances risk (not capital) across asset classes. Stressed when stocks/bonds correlations turn positive (inflation regime).
framework rule
Fundamental principle: a solo signal = noise, 3+ aligned signals = real edge. All panel signals are Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY.
